title: Docker Backed Generated Adapter Conformance
goal: Resolve #426 by adding Docker-backed canonical-runtime conformance for runnable generated adapters.
non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.

intent.outcome: A release-like Docker command can prove runnable generated TypeScript adapter behavior against canonical Python CLI runtime behavior.
intent.constraints: Keep ordinary static checks free of Docker/Node requirements and do not reimplement Python runtime primitives in TypeScript.
intent.latitude: Add an isolated Dockerfile/flag for canonical-runtime conformance and wire proof selection to expose it.
intent.escalation: Escalate when a better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.

execution.next_step: Archive this plan, update external issue evidence, and close #426 with proof.
execution.proof: Run generated package static proof, local Node conformance, Docker package proof, Docker conformance proof, focused tests, workspace tests, ruff, planning doctor, summary, and reconcile.

invariants: Local static generated-package checks still work without Docker.
Docker conformance compares generated adapter behavior to canonical Python CLI behavior.
Failure output remains classified as generation drift, adapter failure, or runtime primitive failure.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Make runnable generated non-Python adapters release-checkable against canonical runtime behavior without expanding ordinary Python development requirements.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Generated package proof can now require Docker-backed canonical-runtime parity for the runnable TypeScript adapter.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: Proof routing still lives in src/agentic_workspace/cli.py, so the direct CLI edit was reviewed as proof-routing metadata rather than runtime behavior.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: Static generated-package proof, local Node conformance, Docker package tests, Docker canonical-runtime conformance, workspace tests, ruff, and planning checks passed.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: Refresh summary and promote another explicit child slice.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Add Docker-backed canonical-runtime conformance for runnable generated adapters.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: No broad generated target parity, no mutation conformance, no TypeScript runtime primitive reimplementation.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Exact flag and Dockerfile shape as long as the canonical-runtime proof is explicit and isolated.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: A better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Preserve the larger intended outcome separately from this bounded slice.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Keep the slice bounded while carrying any larger follow-on through explicit continuation fields.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Did this slice advance the declared larger outcome, or only complete the local task?

active_milestone.scope: Generated TypeScript Docker conformance surface, generated-package check script, proof routing, and focused tests.

immediate_next_action: Archive this plan, update external issue evidence, and close #426 with proof.
invariants: Local static generated-package checks still work without Docker.
Docker conformance compares generated adapter behavior to canonical Python CLI behavior.
Failure output remains classified as generation drift, adapter failure, or runtime primitive failure.
validation_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_contract_tooling.py -q -k "docker_conformance or generated_command_package"
uv run ruff check src tests scripts
uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
completion_criteria: A Docker-backed conformance command proves runnable generated adapter behavior against canonical Python CLI behavior.
Existing static and local conformance checks still pass without requiring Docker for ordinary feedback.
Proof routing advertises the Docker canonical-runtime conformance command for generated package changes.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Added `generated/typescript/Dockerfile.conformance` and `--docker-conformance` so Docker can run the canonical Python runtime parity check for runnable generated TypeScript adapters; proof selection now advertises that command.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --conformance --require-node; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --docker --require-docker; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --docker-conformance --require-docker; uv run pytest tests/test_contract_tooling.py -q -k "docker_conformance or generated_command_package"; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q -k proof_changed_selector_routes_generated_command_packages; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q; uv run ruff check src tests scripts
execution_summary.resume from: Refresh summary and select the next explicit child slice.

proof_report.validation proof: All selected generated package, local conformance, Docker package, Docker canonical-runtime conformance, workspace, lint, and planning proof commands passed.
proof_report.proof achieved now: `--docker-conformance --require-docker` builds `generated/typescript/Dockerfile.conformance` and runs adapter conformance against the canonical Python CLI inside the container.

discard.summary: No full generated-target parity or mutation conformance is needed for #426.

drift_log: 2026-04-27: Promoted from TODO direct-task shape into an execplan.

direct_cli_edit_review.why_direct_edit_was_used: The generated-package proof lane list is currently emitted from the workspace proof/defaults payload in cli.py; this slice changed proof-routing metadata only, not command interface semantics or runtime primitive behavior.
direct_cli_edit_review.follow_on_needed: none for #426; the broader root CLI authority lane remains open in external work evidence.
